block_messages.no_citations: no usable citation keys for mapped papers (bibkey missing or not in ref.bib)
block_messages.title_only_evidence: title-only evidence for this subsection (need abstracts or full text)
block_messages.no_snippets: no evidence snippets extractable from notes/fulltext (need richer abstracts/fulltext)
block_messages.too_few_snippets: too few evidence snippets (need >= {min_needed}; have {actual}); enrich paper notes/evidence bank for this subsection
block_messages.missing_eval_tokens: no concrete evaluation tokens (benchmarks/metrics) extractable for this subsection
block_messages.too_few_claim_candidates: too few snippet-derived claim candidates (need >= {min_needed}); enrich paper notes/evidence bank for this subsection
block_messages.too_few_comparisons: too few concrete comparisons (need >= {min_needed} grounded comparisons backed by clusters/snippets)
block_messages.insufficient_grouping: comparison structure is too weak to ground A-vs-B cards (need richer clusters or more addressable snippets)

downgrade_messages.abstract_only: evidence is mostly abstract-level for this subsection; keep comparative claims conservative unless protocol details are directly supported.
downgrade_messages.insufficient_limitations: limitations/failure-mode evidence is sparse for this subsection; keep comparative claims conservative and prioritize richer extraction upstream.
downgrade_messages.numeric_without_protocol: some quantitative evidence lacks enough protocol context; prefer qualitative wording until task/metric/constraint details are verified.

evaluation_protocol_defaults: When comparing results, anchor the paragraph with: task type + metric + constraint (budget, tool access, horizon, or threat model) when stated.
Prefer head-to-head comparisons only when benchmark/metric are shared; otherwise frame differences as protocol-driven rather than method superiority.
Avoid underspecified model/baseline naming; if abstracts omit details, state that the baseline is reported but underspecified instead of guessing.
If a claim relies on a single reported number, pair it with a limitation/caveat from the same evidence so the draft remains conservative.
If budgets or environments differ across papers, treat cross-paper numeric comparison as fragile and prefer qualitative contrasts aligned to the subsection axes.
